title: Product Brief - June 26, 2026
author: John Vars
content:
---

## Intro

John Vars 

We're closing the books on Q2 this week, and I'm proud of where we landed. We completed a lot of what we set out to do on the roadmap, and we did it while absorbing a heavy load of urgent, unplanned work and pushing through a handful of projects that turned out to be far bigger than anyone scoped. That combination is hard, and the teams handled it. Next week we'll grade ourselves honestly against the roadmap we committed to at the start of the quarter. I'd rather we measure ourselves and learn than pretend every quarter goes to plan.

**Q3 is nearly locked.** [The roadmap](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1gUXJU7bJNUKOyFdiOO5tTqXpZGl3BfbCtGN2319pOF8/edit?slide=id.g383ab8e50d8_0_961#slide=id.g383ab8e50d8_0_961) is almost final after last week's executive review, which was largely confirmatory with a few adjustments. It's built around the Q3 Company Rocks that touch product: **Enterprise Enablement; Onboarding & Trust; Develop & Sell Automations, AI Employees, Vibe; and Corp Dev/Digital Partnerships.** If those sound familiar, they should. They're the sharpened, committed version of the themes we've been circling for the last couple of briefs. The crisp definitions of done, metrics, and deadlines will land shortly.

**Better process, plus the AI moment.** One rock I'm personally taking on is improving our core processes. There's real room to get better across the whole Product org (Product Management, Design, and Support) and in how we work with other groups, our technical vendors, and our enterprise clients. I'm convinced that tightening our processes _while_ we fully exploit the AI moment is what unlocks the next inflection point for Vendasta. Good process isn't bureaucracy. It's what lets us move fast without falling over.

**On Support.** I raised this at All Hands and I want to repeat it here, because it was our top priority at this week's exec meeting: Support has been under serious strain, and that's been painful for customers and for our own people. It was a perfect storm. We moved off Zendesk to vTickets quickly, vTickets is not yet enterprise grade, several migrations generated more tickets than usual, we lost some support staff, we had outages, and more. The team has been working incredibly hard, but we're still backed up and our customers are feeling it, and I won't sugarcoat that. Here's what we're doing about it: adding Brent to the vTickets team to stabilize and improve; hiring more support agents; cross-training two DR customer service reps for Broadly support; and standing up a dedicated support queue for our top 25 enterprise clients alongside a prioritized premium customer list, among other actions. Next week I'll be working with Brent and the team to put real milestones and timelines around this, so we can set clear expectations on the path back to the great support our customers count on.

There's a lot of strong work in this brief, much of it already pointing straight at those Q3 goals. Enjoy this week's Product Brief.

---

## Chief Architect Update

Jesse Redl  

There have been a couple of fun new releases in Vibe that should greatly increase the quality of output. 

### Sessions

A session allows you to have a focused discussion with AI about a specific topic. This enhances the context you can bring to Vibe and build against a focused topic. Finished building? Create a new session and start creating with your next focused objective. 

All of a projects messages are still visible under the all messages area under the session selector. 

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=98ada6252e3b&id=36e7903b-61ef-4549-bc73-b95f94ca00cb&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=669&occurrenceKey=null&width=557&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
### Ask Vibe

A new mode of vibe has launched that allows you have a back and forth discussion with Vibe. When you are ready to build it, switch modes to plan or build. The context / understanding you have built will carry forward into the AI turn.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=441aeaddd369&id=9799ec31-34e0-400a-a936-66ca4bf689f2&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=1265&occurrenceKey=null&width=647&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
‌

### Coming Soon

In the next week or so we will have a couple of fun new aspects of Vibe to explore. 

#### Project Export / Import

As resellers are building client based applications in their own account they are asking for the ability to move a vibe project into another location. Import from a template / export will require a standard+ Vibe subscription.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=fbb5b9cc1ac2&id=ca510d82-eee4-4349-aef9-435681f052b0&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=1858&occurrenceKey=null&width=2916&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
### Queued Conversations

Have a couple of ideas for the same session you want to submit? Queue them up and walk away. Vibe will automatically queue your item for the next session

### CRM Connector

In a closed trusted release you will soon be able to connect Vibe directly to your CRM Contact / Company and Opportunity data. That means the same data within the Business APP CRM can be interacted with, within a Vibe created application just by toggling on the CRM connector. 

### Temporal Contract Renewal

Underway are two key Vendor contract negotiations. As our usage of Temporal has increased (temporal is a fault tolerant workflow orchestration engine that powers much of the platform) so has our temporal cost. Thanks to Parker Reese Shawn Gryschuk and the Autobots team we have greatly reduced our temporal baseline as we move into our next contract period. 

On June 23rd they released a new way of “pre filtering” an automation before we start a temporal workflow. This looks to have reduced the number of temporal workflows triggered by about 70%.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=dda56ce4a1ec&id=d22d1937-c732-4163-b3eb-51150cac51b7&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=617&occurrenceKey=null&width=1647&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
### Looker Contract Renewal

Vendasta uses looker in 2 places in our platform. Our corporate metrics and reporting and our integrated platform reporting (premium reports). 

Aside from overall cost reduction, we are seeking 2 key outcomes:

* Our first contract period assumed we would take premium reports into Business App. This greatly inflated our contracted user number and our product strategy never took this into consideration. We are focusing our in platform reporting to be entirely based upon reseller reporting. 
* The cost of a developer seat within looker is about $100 per month. With the advent of AI we want to enable more people to be able to create looker reports but that means a greatly reduced cost per seat. 

At this time, we have effectively issued Google a contract cancellation for each looker instance until we re-align our Looker expectations in this new world of AI. 

---

## AI Experience

Joshua Baker 

Hey there 👋 Watch our sprint review here → \[[RECORDING: sprint review, Jun 26](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1uAtGAmNDMQD9U7AaFg7mtazSKaCBAXkX/view)\]

**We closed out a big Q2 — crossing $10M ARR in AI employees sold and $5.23M in Conversations AI — and this sprint the Q3 pivot to onboarding and activation got real, with Project Ignite and a first in-app Business App Assistant you can actually chat with.**

### Metrics update

Coming to the end of Q2, it's worth looking back at what the company delivered — both the planned roadmap and a heavy load of injected work that wasn't planned but shipped anyway. A few numbers that capture the quarter:

* **$10M ARR in AI employees sold** — crossed this quarter. Kudos to all product, marketing, sales & ops teams for this solid growth.
* **$5.23M ARR in Conversations AI**, with **+13K MRR added in just the last two weeks**.
* **40M+ automation runs** — real work flowing through the platform.
* **Voice AI churn quietly fixed.** A few sprint reviews ago premium voice churn sat in the 6–7% range with some quality complaints. The team has been steadily tuning voice in the background and it shows: premium churn is now in the low 4s, and we're at \~3% across all SKUs.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=75e7ae239a26&id=d1f26e62-cbbc-4759-ad6c-286365b65c28&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=593&occurrenceKey=null&width=1165&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
The big focus now heading into Q3 is Onboarding: we're **selling** AI employees well, but have a huge activation and engagement gap. In Q3, we’re going to make sure the businesses who are sold our AI workforce (or DIY buy) actually get into Business App, set things up, and see value (the agentic-work-unit you'll see a lot more about next quarter).

In May we added \~4,200 new paying SMBs through the partner channel, but only \~2,600 of them ever logged in. That gap between _paying_ and _showing up_ is the whole opportunity — and this sprint we started making progress towards closing that gap.

_NOTE these are Claude generated analytics and may not be perfectly accurate, but directionally seem correct:_

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=95360a34e739&id=2f5c7f0d-4b65-47c7-9904-bd325ae17b53&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=398&occurrenceKey=null&width=651&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
### Goal: Get SMBs into Business App and to first value (Project Ignite)

Project Ignite is the push to pull more paying SMBs into Business App, get them activated, and give them reasons to upgrade — instead of leaving value sitting behind a login they never use.

**"Here's what your AI already did for you" homepage card** IN PROGRESS A new Business App home card that greets a freshly signed-up SMB with the work already done on their behalf — reviews pulled, a 24-hour visibility scan, pages scanned, even a published website they can preview and edit — before they've configured anything. The idea is to make the first thirty seconds feel like value _already delivered_, not a setup checklist.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=13bcabb28ed8&id=abc6e10d-e1c0-4251-9eb9-5a05b67d62c6&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=448&occurrenceKey=null&width=1417&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**A welcome email worth opening** IN PROGRESS The old welcome email ("set your password") inspired no one to log in. The new onboarding email leads with what the AI has already accomplished — researched the business, started the visibility scan, read the website, built a landing page — with links straight into the work, and localization so it can send in the user's language. Triggered on user creation or on demand from the account page.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=d8427f865981&id=dd313d90-3631-48bf-a79b-26647dd207c5&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=690&occurrenceKey=null&width=955&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**A sharper "get started" experience** IN PROGRESS Reworked the getting-started page to surface the next steps an SMB should take, dismissible as they go, and disappearing once there's nothing left — designed to sit right under the new home card so the whole first session points somewhere.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=bf290ed470d3&id=8fad0fcf-e058-4cb3-b0a3-fc9133bb6488&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=303&occurrenceKey=null&width=1394&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**Sign up with Microsoft in Business App** IN PROGRESS Added Microsoft as a sign-up option into Business App (sign-up done, sign-in in review, behind a flag for now). Microsoft sign-ups are fewer than Google or email, but they convert at a much higher rate and carry higher revenue per account — worth meeting those users where they are.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=ec5ab9adb3f2&id=15961c9c-ecf4-4e35-9a2a-a31499760ab4&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=469&occurrenceKey=null&width=505&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**Up Next:** Wire these pieces into a single onboarding flow — trigger the Vibe project, send the email, land them on a home that shows the work and the next step — and start measuring the effect on activation and time-to-first-value. The team is also designing a standardized "here's what your AI did for you" feed so this isn't one-off cards but a real data model.

**Business App Assistant — chat from inside Business App** IN PROGRESS  To help SMBs not only succeed in onboarding and setup, but throughout their ongoing usage of Business App, we’re adding a partner-managed AI assistant to Business App. Appears as a floating widget, side-docked, and full-screen modes, and its state is global and persistent — it follows you around the app and is still there when you close and reopen it. Very much a work in progress on the UI, but the core is real. From here the work branches into two streams: making the Business App Assistant genuinely great (in close partnership with the DALE team), and turning the underlying chat component into one reusable surface so our various AI chats stop drifting apart in look and feel.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=2a0affe431a3&id=25090bb7-648b-496e-8f4c-ce0570ba071c&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=969&occurrenceKey=null&width=1740&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
‌

### Goal: A robust, feature-rich Conversations inbox

**Multi-location web chat — released to PRBS** TRUSTED TESTER One widget that lists a brand's locations and, once a visitor picks one, becomes a normal web chat wired to that location's AI assistant. This shipped to PRBS this week — a much bigger build than expected, with a lot of care taken to release something stable. This sprint added the configuration preview (welcome greeting, per-location AI message or disable, colours, light/dark), and a "select all / clear all" with an eligibility check so locations without active Conversations AI can't be selected by mistake. Scoped for smaller brands; touch-ups continue as feedback comes in.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=b711faa679ce&id=bb3dc0ef-fc71-4f54-80f0-8fe76d89fe74&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=846&occurrenceKey=null&width=1227&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**Close anonymous conversations** RELEASED GA Anonymous web-chat conversations can now be closed, so a combined inbox view doesn't pile up with noise. This unblocks a real internal use case: our own support team is now running live web-chat conversations with anonymous visitors, and needed a clean way to wrap them up.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=00bae9d18650&id=876cf8f3-4e06-4d00-b224-07cbc70495df&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=647&occurrenceKey=null&width=1447&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**Summaries in the customer's language** RELEASED GA Conversation summaries now follow a business's preferred operating language instead of guessing from location (which broke for, say, a Spanish-speaking business in the US). If the new field is set in Business profile, we use it; if not, we fall back to using the primary language of the city the business is located in. Works across Business App and voice.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=398b8b2852c0&id=abebf766-0a76-4cf7-b76e-0bda09e70d9f&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=425&occurrenceKey=null&width=515&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**GChat for internal teams** IN PROGRESS Long-requested: connect our platform's AI workforce to a Google Chat space so we can put it to work on our own jobs at Vendasta. You can now add a GChat connection behind a flag, and the app can add/remove itself to spaces, view them, and post. Next: let an AI employee actually do something in a channel, like answering support questions. Soon, we can replace all the bots in Vendasta spaces with AI employees that our teams can fully manage and configure and create custom capabilities on.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=166bb81cf156&id=2afed9e2-854c-43a6-95ea-2a05b5dfceaa&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=463&occurrenceKey=null&width=1270&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**Up Next:** Roll everyone over to the filtered inbox and retire the old unfiltered one; enable B2B inbox summaries (so VMF↔partner conversations can kick off automations); and start designing AI-assisted spam detection and important-message flagging so the inbox helps you triage, not just read.

‌

### Goal: AI receptionist — outbound, better voice, and Italian expansion

**Outbound calling (technical MVP)** IN PROGRESS Both voice receptionists and custom assistants can now place outbound AI calls, and we added voice options to custom assistants so they sound right when they do. 

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=585cf9d50fe4&id=5ce171b9-4f84-4375-bb1e-a1e2557f1dab&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=772&occurrenceKey=null&width=1133&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
With a new automation step available on a feature flag, the plumbing for AI outbound calling works; packaging, tiering, and guardrails are the next hurdle:

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=247866402d83&id=ae210d5c-60d1-4657-9484-fcdd5cf78ba0&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=285&occurrenceKey=null&width=569&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
‌

**See which AI handled the call** RELEASED GA Every call now logs to the contact's activity feed with the assistant that handled it shown as the activity owner — picture and name — so a business can see its AI workforce's calls alongside everything else. This work for any CRM activity too – if generated by an AI cit an now be attributed to the AI that performed it; surfacing those avatars in the inbox is in progress.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=c81318f70d99&id=935863d6-21b9-49fe-9d0e-ebf84e6ada4d&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=1598&occurrenceKey=null&width=2816&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
‌

**ElevenLabs support for (almost) any voice** IN PROGRESS Customers will be able to pick any non-paid ElevenLabs voice for their receptionist (we're holding the paid voices back to avoid the surcharges). 

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=482e7831af3e&id=60e376cc-01bf-49a4-bd80-185a4dabdcaa&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=331&occurrenceKey=null&width=1035&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**Real-time voice 2.0** TRUSTED TESTER  Upgraded OpenAI’s real-time voice from 1.5 to 2.0, currently on for VMF. Anyone who wants to test real-time before we roll it out broadly can reach out and we'll enable it.

**Italian number provisioning, automated** IN PROGRESS Twilio still has no number provisioning API for Italy, so buying a number after bundle approval has been a script run by hand to fill out a form. We're replacing our human flow with our own tooling so approved bundles get numbers provisioned ‘automatically’. Paired with this, the registration UI now shows a clear fourth state — "number provisioned, you can make and receive calls and send alphanumeric SMS" — plus accurate states for "still requesting" and "failed, here's why," ending the guessing between _approved_ and _actually working_. There was also a lot of white-glove Twilio work this week, going submission-by-submission with our rep to clear stuck registrations.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=8833a0416ad2&id=44279181-c6af-481d-a66b-7dd0c61560d3&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=730&occurrenceKey=null&width=1497&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
### Update on A2P & Phone/SMS Registration

A lot of people across Vendasta — and our partners — can feel that phone and SMS registration isn't flowing the way it should. It's getting senior, daily attention. 

**United States — rejection rates are high.** 

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=8c977ad810db&id=0029c0ae-4be7-4bb5-a98e-13af862c638c&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=391&occurrenceKey=null&width=958&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
When a business signs up for SMS, its details go to first to Twilio internal team before reaching the phone carriers for approval. Right now a large share of those approvals come back rejected (by Twilio’s pre-review team) — for these reasons:

1. **Submission imperfections –** The paperwork is almost never perfect.  Reviewers reject on the smallest mismatch — a name, an address, a missing word — and most businesses don't get it exactly right the first time. 
2. **Twilio false rejections** - There is a human element on Twilio side where some exactly-the-same franchise submissions are approved, some are denied. The reasons are still unclear, and the blame points to Twilio’s review team.

**What’s amplifying the issue – Customers keep resubmitting things that will never pass, and we pay for the resubmission.** 

Every rejection costs us again, and Twilio surfaces problems one at a time. A rejected application has to be resubmitted from scratch — and we pay on each attempt. Because Twilio's reviewers often flag only the first issue and stop, a business fixes that one thing, resubmits, pays, and gets bounced on the next. Across many businesses, and adding the non-deterministic review process, the cost piles up with little approved to show for it.

The single biggest thing slowing us down: **Twilio doesn’t record exactly _why_ each registration was rejected.** So every fix & resubmission is partly blind. This is our top request to Twilio, they need to do better. 

–

**Italy — the sharp end right now, and escalated.** We're in near-daily, submission-by-submission sessions with our Twilio rep, with the account escalated on their side. The pattern we're seeing: a large share of rejections are _false negatives_ — documents that should pass — bouncing on Italian name order (family name first), business-name punctuation (e.g. "SRL" spelled out), or a sole-proprietor's bill mailed to a different address than the service, even when the business is clearly legitimate. Twilio has already conceded a batch (\~15) were wrongly rejected and is approving them. 

**Two things compound it:** reviewers flag the _first_ error and stop, so we fix it, resubmit (and pay again), and get rejected for the next thing; and Italy sits under a far stricter regulatory regime than the US, so every submission faces a tighter microscope. Too many hands in the chain — the SMB sends docs to ItaliaOnline, their team submits, our account manager guides — adds avoidable errors, which is exactly what the [Italian document Tips guide](https://vendasta.jira.com/wiki/spaces/AIX/pages/4336582755) is built to prevent. 

**What we’re doing to improve things:**

* Vendastians are meeting with Twilio daily, and reviewing failed submissions in detail.  

    * We’re pushing them to be better, and stop false negative failures. 
    
* We’re considering paying for Twilio’s “Fast Track” enterprise tier, which gives us a dedicated person on their internal team at manually reviews submissions, has influence with carriers, and can ‘push through’ registrations.
* We're going to eliminate the USA A2P cost of resubmission by using the "Update" API instead of "Submit" API. [https://vendasta.jira.com/browse/BREW-2745](https://vendasta.jira.com/browse/BREW-2745)
* There are additional Twilio error messages we can get and display to end users in the UI for USA A2P, we're going to add more. [https://vendasta.jira.com/browse/BREW-2746](https://vendasta.jira.com/browse/BREW-2746)
* Improvements to reporting add transparency – Here’s [Italy Report](https://datastudio.google.com/u/0/reporting/9f193f7a-33c3-490b-baa3-85eb63b610b2/page/TlJ0C), and the [A2P USA Report](https://vendastainternal.cloud.looker.com/dashboards/751?Vendasta+Provider=Yes&Is+Billable+%28Yes+%2F+No%29=Yes&Is+Internal+%28Yes+%2F+No%29=No%2CYes&Partner+ID=&Partner+Name=&Name=&Account+ID=)
* We’ve drafted an updated “Tips” doc for Italy submissions specifically, https://vendasta.jira.com/wiki/spaces/AIX/pages/4336582755 – we’ll share this with IOL, but might consider adding this into the forms directly.
* We also have been thinking about how an AI Assistant could help a business get their submission right the first try.

‌

### Goal: Build automations by chatting — and run them leaner

**Automation cost cut roughly in half** RELEASED  We were spending real money in Temporal just to evaluate a trigger and then throw the event away. We moved all of that — trigger filters, conditions, run settings — to _before_ Temporal, so we only pay for orchestration when we're actually doing something for the user. First full day after the change, usage dropped from the \~30–40M range to \~17–19M. We also added a rate limit on the meeting-bot scheduler so repeated clicks can't quietly book (and bill for) duplicate bots.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=4d5c52b89cd1&id=64588ea6-389f-4ef1-a1b3-39c021a11583&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=659&occurrenceKey=null&width=1886&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**AI automation builder — smarter and self-aware** IN PROGRESS The describe-it-and-watch-it-build experience keeps maturing. The builder now knows which platform tools it actually has (CRM tools included) and lists them so it stops asking for things it can't do, and the canvas now refreshes and re-focuses on the step it just changed instead of leaving you to reload. CRM record lookup inside the builder is in progress. We also stopped truncating large automation payloads — enterprise teams can now see the full inbound and outbound payload to spot a wrong key or value.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=d341689c4552&id=293a8e5c-70f8-4505-af3e-c53e0981eb67&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=842&occurrenceKey=null&width=1383&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
**Automation versioning (design + RFC)** IN PROGRESS Mocked what version history could look like: preview past versions and their step config, see who toggled a version on or off and when, restore, or branch a draft and publish it as a new version. It solves a genuine support headache — when an "it's broken" ticket lands, the automation is often already running, and version history tells us which version actually caused the problem. An RFC is out, and the aim is a model consistent enough to reuse for Vibe and AI employees.

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=84dfb911b8a0&id=2b3fcf1d-685e-45ad-a51c-757c156d7944&&collection=contentId-4332716108&height=656&occurrenceKey=null&width=552&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)Mock of individual automation version history
‌

**Up Next:** Give partners control over the automations they deploy down to their SMBs, lay in automation credits, and support Vibe apps with automation playbooks — with versioning first, since it unblocks both our teams and partner teams.

—

A strong close to a migration-heavy Q2. The SMB activation metrics make the next goal obvious — we sell AI employees and receptionists well, and now the whole game is getting the businesses who buy them set up and seeing value fast. That's what Project Ignite and the Business App Assistant are the opening moves of. It's our [program North Star](https://vendasta.jira.com/wiki/spaces/AIX/pages/2036139376), and Q3 starts now.

Huge thanks to the WOW, Autobots, TIF, and SnackOps teams for a genuinely beefy sprint — and to everyone who demoed.

Thanks for the read!

— Josh

## Platform (CRM, Billing & Integration)

Gabriel Tsoi 

Rodrigo Carvalho and I just wrapped up 3 days at the IoL Milan office, and the team here gave us an incredibly warm welcome. They actually tricked us into ordering cappuccinos right after lunch, and the waiter gave us the side-eye on cue. Total callback to the joke Umberto made with BK about the exact same thing last time he was here!

On the business side, IoL is driving impressive sales momentum. They’ve already captured over 300 MARIO activations across both their legacy CRM (Inrete) and the new Business App CRM (Inrete AI). On top of that, more than 1,000 businesses are already on the Inrete AI solutions, with 70% on the higher tier. After this initial rollout, they’re now at a critical inflection point for ramping up sales and operations, and they need our help to clear the runway.

To keep a long story short, we spent the week systematically tackling the operational friction they've hit while rolling these solutions out to their client base. We fixed the Italy-specific CSV template issues, blocked out agent login and PII blockers, and found a short-term fix for MARIO's accuracy on multi-PDF menus. We also resolved their KPI issues, which turned out to be a mix of an IoL implementation error, a minor API bug, and some needed change requests around field mapping and source translation and other minor issues.

To keep everyone aligned moving forward, we consolidated separate priority lists from different IoL teams into a [single, prioritized Jira backlog ](https://vendasta.jira.com/jira/core/projects/IOLEXT/issues?jql=project+%3D+IOLEXT+AND+type+in+%28Sub-task%2C+Task%29+ORDER+BY+created+DESC&atlOrigin=eyJpIjoiMjZkMzMxNjNkOWY4NDUyMTliOTdlNDU1ODFlYzRkZTYiLCJwIjoiaiJ9)and got their team onboarded. Finally, a huge thanks to Ashwin Subrahmanyam  for helping us get our Chennai team (Arun Pandian Kavitha M and Sangeetha K) onboarded onto the IoL project this week!

**Next Steps: Heading to Turin**

Next week, we’re heading to Turin to shadow their front-line operations teams. We'll be focusing on MARIO on Tuesday and Thursday, and Inrete AI on Wednesday.

There is a lot to digest here, from how the problems IoL faces exactly mirror our own, to how much we're both learning about managing these processes at scale. We're also navigating how quickly they pivot based on market signals, and looking at why things can still feel slow on the ground despite adding headcount to support them.

We'll share a much more detailed write-up covering all of these key takeaways and operational reflections right after the trip, so stay tuned!

On the product side, we closed out a migration-heavy Q2 on a high note, with the beta of Developer Portal 2.0, the LinkedIn Ads integration heading into trusted tester, and the most-requested CRM AI Pro capability, in-person meeting recording, all landing this sprint. Shout out to Will Fawcett for facilitating and organizing the sprint review on Thursday!

### trusted tester Developer Portal 2.0 beta, collecting feedback

We released the beta of Developer Portal 2.0, rebuilt on Docusaurus and organized into Getting Started, Guides, and API References. The big unlock is how we surface APIs: our internal gRPC services are now exposed as auto-generated REST proxies through the build pipeline, rather than hand-written into API gateways. Legacy endpoints are clearly separated from the modern Vendasta APIs, with a plan to consolidate the legacy set and steer developers toward the new ones. The beta lives on a standalone URL for now and will eventually fold into docs.vendasta.com.

### trusted tester LinkedIn Ads lead sync into the CRM

The LinkedIn Ads integration is in its final stages and heading into trusted tester shortly. Users authenticate via **SSO** with their LinkedIn Campaign Manager, choose an ad account, and leads created in LinkedIn sync directly into the Vendasta CRM. Current scope is centered on lead sync; we can expand into additional LinkedIn data as customer demand dictates, since each capability needs its own access grant from LinkedIn. We will coordinate with the Vendasta Ads team to keep the experience consistent with Ad Intel’s existing LinkedIn connector.

### In progress Integrations as a Tool Authentication Method

A meaningful win for AI builders: SMBs and partners can now select a connected **integration** as the authentication method for a custom capability’s tools, instead of manually pasting and maintaining the lifecycle of an API key. In the demo, a HubSpot context-fetch tool worked end to end just by checking a box and selecting the integration. This removes real friction for anyone building custom AI capabilities.

### trusted tester CRM AI: In-Person Meeting Recording

The highlight of the sprint. We brought audio file upload and live meeting recording to the My Meetings page, mirroring the contact-page workflow. On mobile, floating action buttons let a rep record on the spot (pause, resume, scrub, change speed, rename) and save against a selected contact. This is the **nu**mber one feature request for CRM AI Pro, and the home-service space is full of businesses that want it. We have plans to surface the record button in more locations so a rep already inside a contact can simply hit record.

Meeting analysis was extended further: speaker assignment and reassignment on the UI for audio recordings (with auto-detection and unassigned-speaker flagging), generalized shareable links using a `meeting` prefix so audio and video share one structure, a format-aware audio/video player on the public recording page, and Sales Coach tab parity for audio recordings.

### Yesware Replatforming

* trusted tester **Tracked opens and links:** reps can toggle open and click tracking per email, with events surfacing as CRM activities to time follow-ups. First stage of a broader effort spanning opens, links, attachments, and replies.
* trusted tester **Email connection quality of life:** connections now show profile picture, name, and email, and users are restricted to connecting each Gmail or Outlook account only once (enforced via backfill). Auto-draft replies were teased as upcoming work.
* trusted tester **Standalone emails and campaigns:** select an existing email into a campaign or create from a template or blank, send standalone emails with full clicks-and-opens stats that combine across campaigns, and choose the email footer language.

### trusted tester File Management Toward a Universal CMS

We continued improving the file management system built for CRM and intended to be platform-wide. Recent additions: advanced sharing (the clearer “Shared with” model), advanced search and filters, and AI-generated document descriptions that make files searchable by their contents. The social marketing team is adopting it as their CMS foundation, and Vibe is already using it.

### Italiaonline Enablement

* **Alphanumeric SMS without a Twilio regulatory bundle:** Italian businesses can send SMS campaigns using an alphanumeric sender ID, removing the compliance-form step previously required to obtain a real Italian number.
* **Italian translation fixes:** corrected translations across the date picker and the CRM contacts table.

### Enterprise Enablement and Paper Cuts

* Released **Automation status in account templates:** see whether a template’s automations are active before applying.
* trusted tester **Ticket workplace:** persistent view-filter sidebar plus Chrome-style tabs for tickets, so support agents move quickly without re-filtering. In progress, aimed at VMF.
* Released **Safeguarding CRM delete:** bulk deletes require a typed confirmation, and system-namespace field options are locked from customization. An accelerated item for DaySmart.
* Released **Campaign SMS in the inbox:** fixed so campaign SMS reappears in conversations, and added date-range filters to the campaign activity page.
* Released **AI credits page gating:** page hidden and URL-redirected for accounts without an entitled product.
* Internal **GoDaddy support tooling:** super-admin domain lookup, transfer instructions, renewals, and shopper edits.
* trusted tester **Order PDF generation:** one combined back-end PDF with all line items, totals, order forms, and customer notes.

That wraps a high-output sprint and a strong close to a migration-heavy Q2, from the Developer Portal and LinkedIn Ads work to the meeting recording capability our home-service businesses have been asking for. Huge thanks to the MIT, Psychobillers, Marina, Phoenix, NP-easy, Meerkats, Snapcats and Honeybadger teams for the push. As we move into Q3, we will keep the momentum on Yesware replatforming and the Broadly wind-down while staying close to enterprise partners like ItaliaOnline, because the more we help them win, the more we sharpen the platform for everyone. More to come after Turin! - Gabriel

## Vendasta Ads (formerly known as Matchcraft)

Matt Kraft 

### **Ads in Partner Center (Ad Planner)**

A big sprint for Ad Planner, with a wave of enhancements aimed at proposal quality, pricing transparency, and overall usability based on what we're learning from both internal teams and Fast Track partners. Plus, we had our first instance of a campaign being purchased by a channel partner (Janice Christopher Marketing Agency / JCA)!

On the ad plan quality side, we extended the **custom instructions** capability. Users could already tune ad copy and keywords via an optional instructions field, and now they can tune the **budget proposal** too, specifying a target budget, how many options to show, and more. This is handy for partners who have a fixed budget amount in mind up front.

We also fixed a long-standing annoyance with **Google Display plans**: sometimes we would previously suggest too many images for a responsive display ad to be valid, forcing users to manually remove some before saving. Plans now come back valid from the start. Additionally, when a user adjusts the radius on a geographic target, the map preview now updates in real time, so it's immediately obvious what impact their change has. Previously the map didn't update until after saving the proposal.

We also added more **transparency around call tracking fees**. When checking the call tracking box during plan creation, it's now clear that a fee is associated with it, and the price shows up more prominently in the Prepare Order flow as well. This should reduce confusion given that we always charge for call tracking in Ad Planner, whereas the first line was sometimes free in previous Partner Center ad SKUs.

A few workflow and access improvements rounded out the sprint. We discovered that the new "Ads" tab in Partner Center wasn't visible by default to users with the **salesperson role**, so we fixed that. They can now see and use the feature. We made the **shareable preview link** accessible directly from the campaign proposal, removing the awkward save-and-navigate step partners previously had to go through to pivot between the editable proposal and the SMB-facing view. And in response to early usage from AI Fast Track partners, we added an **actions column in the ad plans table to support deleting plans**, so partners can keep their plans organized.

We also did a small cleanup to **strip unwanted tracking parameters** pulled in from the SMB's account before they get provisioned to the plan and campaign.

### **Connect**

This sprint was focused on expanding our API surface for partners and improving outcomes for campaigns in more currencies.

We added **TikTok and LinkedIn operations to the Roar Connect API documentation**. A couple of partners, including Italiaonline and iPromote, are eager to understand how they can build integrations with these features, so publishing the docs now lets them start evaluating the offering. Relatedly, we enabled **uploading responsive display ad creatives via URL through the Roar Connect API**, a specific need from Italiaonline. Previously they had to upload images through a separate endpoint and then reference the resource name when creating ad ad, so this saves several calls and reduces complexity. It also brings feature parity with the legacy standard account Roar API.

On the bidding side, we **expanded our initial bid algorithm to support Euros and Australian Dollars** (it previously covered USD, CAD, and GBP). Campaigns spending in these currencies will now ramp up faster, since we start with a higher bid tuned to the specific business rather than starting from $0.05. This cuts down the time needed for bid price discovery and is crucial when it comes to making a positive first impression for new SMBs. This has been a priority for Italiaonline, so we’re excited to finally bring the feature to Italy.

### **R&D Alignment**

We switched to using the **automatically generated TypeScript SDK in Galaxy**. That’s it.

## Technology

Megan Cheesbrough  

### D.A.L.E. & AI Infrastructure

This is the last sprint of Q2, so it’s a natural checkpoint against the threads we’ve been reporting all quarter. Cross-namespace knowledge sharing reached trusted tester, the first slice of human-in-the-loop approvals is built (release is the next step), and our testing and quality work moved forward on two separate fronts: scheduled test runs, and success evaluations that score real conversations.

**Sharing Knowledge Across Accounts (Trusted Tester)**

A partner can now group the knowledge an AI Employee learns from (the websites and files it draws on) into a collection and share that collection down to specific account groups. It applies read-only, and the partner who shared it stays in control of what’s shared and with whom. The point is managing that knowledge in one place instead of re-loading the same material account by account, which matters most for partners whose brands or verticals span many accounts, and for cases where a new account group should inherit standard, centrally-managed Vendasta knowledge like white-label support content.

This is at trusted tester now, and we’d like more testers. If you work with partners who have brand- or vertical-spanning knowledge, or you have an internal case for inheriting standard Vendasta knowledge, send them our way.

**AI Employees Can Pause and Ask a Human to Approve an Action**

An AI Employee can pause before a high-consequence action (editing a record, publishing, deleting) and ask a person to approve or reject it before it goes ahead. It’s the kind of human-control step partners kept raising in PAC. The first slice handles approvals from logged-in users with a known identity, so it appears in platform chats where there’s actually someone identifiable to grant it. The approval step itself is built; we’re reworking the interface into a simpler conversational prompt before it ships, so release is the next step.

**AI Test Suites Can Run Automatically on a Schedule**

An AI Employee’s test suite can now run automatically on a schedule, daily or weekly, with a ping in Google Chat when behaviour drifts past a threshold. Test runs also got their own lower rate limits, so a test run can’t eat into the request and token budget a live assistant needs. It’s a milestone toward putting AI testing in partners’ hands.

**Success Evaluations: a Real-World Quality Signal**

A success evaluation looks at a real customer conversation that already happened and scores whether the AI Employee did what it was there to do: book the meeting, answer the question, resolve the issue. Because it’s scored from a live interaction after the fact, it tells us how an employee is actually performing in production. This sprint we added the invocation context an evaluation reads and the filtering that decides which interactions get scored, so we now have a real-world quality signal to build benchmarks and reporting on.

**AI Employees Deactivate With Their Product**

When a customer’s product is deactivated or downgraded, the AI Employee tied to it now turns off with it, so it won’t keep running where the customer no longer has access. The employee’s configuration is preserved, so it comes back intact if the product is reactivated. This controls whether the employee runs; it doesn’t yet gate the individual API tools an employee can call, which is separate work.

### SRE

Between enterprise customers and a steady stream of new AI use cases, the product is scaling fast, and SRE is staying on top of costs so we can keep scaling. By trimming how a Kubernetes container-log dataset is stored in BigQuery, they cut roughly $33K/year (live as of Jun 24), with another \~$49K/year identified from a change to how those logs are billed, pending a confirmation from Google. On the reliability side, the Elasticsearch cluster upgrade we demoed last sprint went into production cleanly, and a routing change that keeps Elastic traffic on the private network (saving CloudNAT cost) is expected in prod early next week.

## New/Updated Product Factory Artifacts

John Vars 

Since April, the Product Factory has built real momentum on the front half of the pipeline. We've produced 55 Mission Briefs, 105 RFCs, and 111 in-scope Epics, and the quality of our discipline-defining artifact, the Mission Brief, is genuinely strong, averaging 7.4 out of 10, with 21 of 55 already driven to a "Supported" decision state rather than languishing in draft. RFCs are technically solid as well at 7.5 out of 10, and stalled work is rare, with only four artifacts left untouched for six or more weeks, which means most in-flight work is actively moving. Where we need to tighten up is the back half of the chain and basic hygiene:

* **Epic quality is low**, scoring just 4.5 out of 10, dragged down by 16 Epics with empty or placeholder descriptions.
* **Label coverage is weak** at only 35 percent, since nearly two-thirds of Epics are unlabeled or carry off-book labels.
* **Artifacts aren't wired together.** Only about 22 percent of Epics link back to a Mission Brief and just 6 percent have a complete chain running from Vision to Brief to RFC to Epic, so excellent individual documents aren't yet connecting into a traceable pipeline.
* **Mission Briefs are missing walk-through videos**, with roughly three-quarters still lacking the now-required video.
* **We rarely kill ideas**, with only a single idea marked "Won't Build" across the entire organization, a sign we may be advancing nearly everything rather than making deliberate decisions.

The priorities for next cycle are clear and low effort: fill in empty Epic descriptions, label every Epic, and require a Mission Brief or RFC link whenever an Epic is opened. I am going to be working with individuals to make sure we are taking care of this basic, but important hygiene.
